other_observers: Brenda Linfield; I believe that I was the original finder of this bird.  Peter Gent arrived not long after I first saw the bird. The bird had gone by then. However, Peter subsequently told me that he refound the bird one hour later.
description: The bird was near the top of an 80' tall deciduous tree, singing loudly.  The song was clearly vireo-like, with a hoarse two-note phrase, a 5 - 10 second pause, and then the two-note phrase again.  The bird was larger than a Warbling Vireo, but with generally similar shape.  It had a bold white belly, unstreaked, and a bright yellow unstreaked breast.  The face showed bright yellow spectacles on a medium background (dark eye, solid medium dark nape and mantle).  The bill was dark, too thick for a warbler.  The wings were dark, but I could not see details from my viewing position.Behaviors: The bird perched near the top of a tall deciduous tree, sang, and then moved to a different perch. It did not move around quickly through foliage in the typical manner of a warbler.Call: The song was clearly vireo-like, with a hoarse two-note phrase, a 5 - 10 second pause, and then the two-note phrase again.Plumage: adult
